title: AI Mastery: Agentic Architecture to Production
subtitle: 12-Week Personal Roadmap — Career + Builds
description: A structured learning program bridging your MDM/governance expertise with cutting-edge agentic AI. Each week produces a tangible artifact for your portfolio or SnapSense.

phases.title: Agentic AI Architecture
phases.goal: Build production-grade multi-agent systems. Strengthens your Re³ narrative and is the #1 topic in Director-level AI interviews right now.

modules.title: LangGraph Foundations
learn: LangGraph state machines — nodes, edges, conditional routing
How LangGraph differs from LangChain DAGs (cyclic vs linear)
ReAct pattern: Reason → Act → Observe loop
State management with TypedDict and reducers

build.title: Research Agent with Self-Correction
build.description: Build an agent that takes a topic, searches for information, evaluates relevance of results, rewrites query if results are poor, and generates a summary. Use Claude API as the LLM via langchain-anthropic.
build.deliverable: Working Python script with a 3-node graph (Router → Searcher → Evaluator) that loops on failure

resources.title: IBM/Coursera — Agentic AI with LangGraph (free audit)
Rahul Kolekar — Agentic RAG with LangGraph (2026)

modules.careerAngle: In interviews, describe this as: 'I built stateful agent workflows with self-correction loops — the agent evaluates its own retrieval quality and retries autonomously.' This maps directly to your Re³ orchestrators (Hypatia, Socratia, Ada).
modules.snapSenseAngle: This is the foundation for SnapSense's future agent layer — routing scans to category-specific handlers with quality checks.

modules.title: Agentic RAG — Self-Correcting Retrieval
learn: Agentic RAG vs Naive RAG — why loops beat pipelines
Document grading with LLM-as-judge
Hallucination checking and query rewriting
ChromaDB / vector store integration

build.title: Full Agentic RAG Pipeline
build.description: Extend Week 1 agent: Router → Retriever → Grader → Generator → Hallucination Checker. If grading fails, auto-rewrite query and loop. Add ChromaDB for document storage.
build.deliverable: Agentic RAG system that self-corrects on retrieval failures and validates its own outputs

resources.title: Rahul Kolekar — Production Agentic RAG Guide

modules.careerAngle: This maps to enterprise use cases like 'AI-powered contract analysis' or 'intelligent supplier onboarding.' Use your MDM domain to frame the story.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Pattern directly applicable to SnapSense's product lookup pipeline — if OFF data is incomplete, rewrite the search or fallback to name-based lookup.

modules.title: Multi-Agent Systems + MCP
learn: Multi-agent orchestration patterns (hierarchical, collaborative, supervisor)
Anthropic's Model Context Protocol (MCP) — universal AI connector standard
Claude Agent SDK — subagents, hooks, tool search
MCP's three primitives: tools, resources, prompts

build.title: 3-Agent Collaboration System
build.description: Build Researcher → Analyst → Writer agent system. Connect to at least one MCP server (filesystem or API wrapper). Each agent has its own tools and state.
build.deliverable: Multi-agent system with MCP integration, demonstrating agent-to-agent communication

resources.title: Anthropic Webinar — Claude Code Advanced Patterns

modules.careerAngle: MCP is becoming the USB-C of AI tool connectivity. Apple added it to Xcode, OpenAI added it to ChatGPT. Knowing MCP positions you as someone who understands the infrastructure layer, not just the prompt layer.
modules.snapSenseAngle: MCP could become SnapSense's integration standard — connect to Open Food Facts, Open Beauty Facts, and ASPCA databases through a single protocol.

modules.title: Capstone — SnapSense Agent Architecture
learn: Applying agentic patterns to real product architecture
Agent cost optimization — model routing, token management
Evaluation: measuring agent quality with LangSmith and custom evals

build.title: SnapSense Agent Layer Design
build.description: Design and prototype an agent layer for SnapSense: multi-step agent that takes a camera scan, routes to category handler, calls external APIs (OFF, OBF), grades data quality, assembles tile response.
build.deliverable: Architecture design doc + working prototype. This is your 'I applied agentic AI to my own product' interview story.

modules.careerAngle: Directors who build > Directors who only strategize. This capstone proves you can take theory to product.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Even if you don't ship this immediately, having the architecture documented positions SnapSense for its next major evolution.

phases.title: AI Governance & Risk Frameworks
phases.goal: Formalize your GIM Framework with industry standards. This is your unique differentiator — most data leaders don't have governance + hands-on AI.

modules.title: NIST AI RMF Deep Dive
learn: Four core functions: Govern, Map, Measure, Manage
NIST AI RMF Generative AI Profile — LLM-specific risks
Mapping NIST AI RMF to your GIM Framework's 5 pillars

build.title: GIM ↔ NIST Crosswalk
build.description: Create a crosswalk document: GIM Framework (58 nodes) mapped to NIST AI RMF functions. Identify gaps and enhancement opportunities. This becomes a portfolio artifact.
build.deliverable: Published crosswalk document — shareable on LinkedIn

resources.title: Certified Info Security — NIST AI RMF Certification

modules.careerAngle: When asked 'How would you implement AI governance?', you answer with NIST functions AND your own GIM framework. That's a 1-2 punch nobody else has.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Document how SnapSense's privacy-by-design (on-device only) maps to NIST's trustworthiness characteristics.

modules.title: ISO 42001 — Certifiable AI Management
learn: ISO 42001 structure (Plan-Do-Check-Act, mirrors ISO 27001)
Key clauses: AI risk assessment, impact assessment, AI policy
Certification process and audit requirements

build.title: AI Management System (AIMS) Policy Draft
build.description: Draft an AIMS policy document for an enterprise. Include: scope, AI risk register, roles/responsibilities, KPIs, continuous improvement process.

resources.title: EC-Council — EU AI Act vs NIST vs ISO 42001

modules.careerAngle: ISO 42001 Lead Implementer certification is rare. Having it immediately signals 'I can operationalize AI governance, not just talk about it.'
modules.snapSenseAngle: N/A — this is purely career-focused but strengthens your overall positioning.

modules.title: EU AI Act + Regulatory Landscape
learn: EU AI Act risk categories (Unacceptable, High, Limited, Minimal)
GPAI model obligations (enforcement Aug 2026)
How US enterprises are preparing without US-specific regulation

build.title: AI Risk Classification Tool
build.description: Build a web app: input an AI use case, get risk tier classification under EU AI Act, required controls from ISO 42001, and mapped NIST AI RMF functions. Claude API powers the classification.
build.deliverable: Working web tool — deployable as a standalone app or Re³ feature

resources.title: CSA — ISO 42001 & NIST for EU AI Act

modules.careerAngle: EU AI Act enforcement starts Aug 2026. Companies are scrambling NOW. You become the person who can lead readiness.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Classify SnapSense itself — it's likely 'Limited Risk' given health-adjacent analysis. Document this proactively.

modules.title: Capstone — GIM Framework 2.0
learn: Synthesize NIST + ISO 42001 + EU AI Act into unified view
Identify what GIM needs to become enterprise-certifiable

build.title: GIM Framework 2.0 Release
build.description: Upgrade GIM to v2.0: NIST AI RMF alignment layer, ISO 42001 control mapping, EU AI Act risk classification. Update Gimmy chatbot. Publish on LinkedIn as thought leadership.

modules.careerAngle: This turns GIM from 'a project I built' into 'an enterprise-grade AI governance framework aligned with international standards.' Director-level statement.

phases.title: On-Device AI + Prompt Engineering at Scale
phases.goal: Directly applicable to SnapSense. Reduce API costs, enable offline classification, and formalize prompt engineering into a repeatable system.

modules.title: Core ML + On-Device Classification
learn: Core ML framework — Apple's ML pipeline
Converting models to .mlmodel format
On-device vs cloud inference trade-offs

build.title: On-Device Image Classifier
build.description: Take a pre-trained model (MobileNet/EfficientNet), convert to Core ML, run in a simple iOS app. Benchmark inference time, accuracy, model size.
build.deliverable: Working iOS app with benchmarks + decision doc for SnapSense implications

modules.careerAngle: On-device AI + privacy-by-design is compelling for healthcare/pharma Director roles. Your Hive local storage constraint already demonstrates this thinking.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Could let SnapSense do category detection without any API call — huge cost and latency win.

modules.title: TensorFlow Lite + Flutter
learn: TFLite for mobile — more relevant for Flutter cross-platform
tflite_flutter plugin integration
Model quantization — shrinking models for mobile

build.title: Cloud vs Edge Benchmark
build.description: Integrate TFLite object detection into Flutter test app. Compare: Cloud Vision API (current SnapSense) vs on-device TFLite inference. Measure latency, accuracy, cost.
build.deliverable: Benchmark report with data-driven architecture recommendation

modules.careerAngle: 'I benchmarked cloud vs edge inference and made a data-driven architecture decision' — shows technical depth beyond typical Director candidates.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Direct input into SnapSense's architecture evolution. Could ship this.

modules.title: Prompt Engineering at Scale
learn: Structured output patterns (JSON mode, XML scaffolding)
Prompt chaining architectures (sequential, parallel, conditional)
Evaluation frameworks: RAGAS, DeepEval
Cost optimization: model routing (Haiku → Sonnet → Opus)

build.title: SnapSense Prompt Evaluation Suite
build.description: Formalize every SnapSense prompt template. Create eval suite (20+ test cases per category). Measure accuracy/cost/latency per model tier. Build A/B testing harness.

modules.careerAngle: Most people 'do prompting.' Very few have a systematic evaluation framework. This shows engineering discipline, not enthusiasm.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Directly improves SnapSense scan quality and reduces API costs.

modules.title: Capstone — Architecture Decision Document
learn: Synthesize all 12 weeks into a coherent narrative
Architecture Decision Records (ADRs) methodology

build.title: SnapSense + Career Architecture Document
build.description: Comprehensive doc covering: (1) Agent layer from Phase 1, (2) AI governance compliance from Phase 2, (3) On-device vs cloud matrix from Phase 3, (4) Prompt evaluation system. This IS your interview portfolio piece.
build.deliverable: The single document that tells your complete story: 'I can build AI products, govern them responsibly, optimize their cost, and make principled architecture decisions.'

modules.careerAngle: This document IS the Director portfolio piece. Period.
modules.snapSenseAngle: Doubles as SnapSense's technical architecture documentation.

certifications.name: NIST AI RMF Architect
certifications.note: Maps directly to GIM + governance positioning
